aids.title: PAC-VIN-SO = Learning Theory Concepts
aids.content: **P**AC learning, **A**ccuracy (epsilon), **C**onfidence (delta), **V**C dimension, **I**nductive bias, **N**o Free Lunch, **S**ample complexity, **O**ccam's Razor — covers all key topics.

aids.title: The Flexible Glove Analogy
aids.content: VC dimension is like measuring how flexible a glove is. A glove that can **shatter** 3 differently-shaped objects (fit every arrangement) has VC=3. If a 4th object exists that the glove cannot accommodate no matter how you try, the VC dimension stays at 3. Linear classifiers in 2D are a 'glove' that fits 3 points but not 4.

aids.title: PAC Quick Check
aids.content: In PAC learning: epsilon = how WRONG the answer can be (error), delta = how often it CAN fail (failure probability). Small epsilon = very accurate. Small delta = very confident. Remember: P(error <= epsilon) >= 1 - delta. The 'probably' is (1-delta), the 'approximately correct' is (epsilon).
aids.forRemembering: PAC learning epsilon and delta parameters
